sections.paragraph:
[יְרֵכָה Thes.] f. i.q. יָרֵךְ No. 2, the hinder part, or side (of a country), Gen 49:13. Compare כָּתֵף, שְׁכֶם.

Dual יַרְכָּתַיִם constr. יַרְכְּתֵי, properly both sides, the haunches, the buttocks, but always used of inanimate things-

(1) the hinder part, the back, the rear, Exo 26:23 of the temple, 1Ki 6:16 Eze 46:23.

(2) in the interior area, the parts farthest in, the extremities, inmost recesses, as of a house, Psa 128:3 of a ship, Jon 1:5 of a cave, 1Sa 24:4 of a sepulchre, Isa 14:15 Eze 39:19. Hence יַרְכְּתֵי לְבָנֹון the recesses of Lebanon, i.e. the extreme and inaccessible parts of that wood. Isa 37:24 and Jud. 19:1 , 18 Jdg 19:18, יַכְּתֵי הַר־אֶפְרַים “the inner recesses of the mountains of Ephraim.” Hence-

(3) the most remote regions. [Is not this meaning wholly needless?] יַרְכְּתֵי צָפֹון the extreme regions of the north, Isa 14:13. [But see Psa 48:3, where the same phrase is applied to Jerusalem, and belongs to the description of its site; although Gesenius contradicts this in Thes., applying it to some other place.] יַרְכְּתֵי אֶרֶץ the extreme regions of the earth, Jer 6:22 25:32 (comp. כִּנְפֹות הָאָרֶץ).

ירכה

f. Chald. the thigh, the haunch, Dan 2:32.
